derived.causal_summary: After a checkpoint-v2 restore, runPostRestoreRebuilds() (mcp_server/lib/storage/checkpoints.ts ~1701-1772) rebuilds derived artifacts (FTS, communities, derived graph) best-effort and catches failures; restoreCheckpointV2() (~2456-2464) calls it after the DB swap and reopen. If a rebuild step fails, the restore still reports success while the derived artifacts stay stale until some later scan re-indexes them. The existing boot FTS auto-heal is tied to the .unclean-shutdown marker (context-server.ts ~364-418), not to checkpoint-restore rebuild failures, and the startup scan (~1385-1477) does not run the checkpoint derived-rebuild helper, so a post-restore rebuild failure leaves a silent stale-derived window. This packet writes a durable .needs-rebuild sentinel when a post-restore derived rebuild fails or is skipped (the restore still succeeds because the base snapshot is valid), then checks it on boot and immediately before the startup scan to run a bounded, non-fatal derived rebuild that clears the sentinel on success. runPostRestoreRebuilds() returns a {completed, failed, skipped} summary and exports one shared derived-rebuild helper reused by restore, boot, and scan; memory-index.ts checks the sentinel under the scan lease (additive and distinct from packet 004's enrichment backfill at ~249-333) and reports repair counts; vector-index-store.ts swap-done journal recovery (~876-925) preserves or creates rebuild-needed state when rebuild evidence is absent. Options weighed: (A) keep existing behavior leaves the stale window (rejected); (B) fail the restore on a rebuild failure is too harsh since the snapshot restored fine (rejected); (C) sentinel plus boot/scan repair is durable and non-blocking (chosen). The follow-up research recommended deferring this, but the operator explicitly authorized implementing it now alongside packet 004. No dist rebuild, no daemon restart, no live-DB access; implemented and unit-tested on a branch only with deploy as a separate confirmed step.
